steps.title: Identify the passage's structure
steps.content: The passage covers three events in sequence: (1) gray wolves were eradicated from Yellowstone in 1926, (2) elk overpopulation and overgrazing followed, (3) scientists recognized the wolf's importance and reintroduced it in 1996 with immediate positive results. The question asks which choice best describes the overall structure.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It summarizes a problem that developed in Yellowstone National Park in the 1920s and then offers potential solutions to that problem.': The passage describes one implemented solution, not multiple potential ones. "Potential solutions" mischaracterizes the text. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It mentions the elimination of the gray wolf from Yellowstone National Park and then explains why the wolf was eventually restored to the park.': Matches the passage exactly — eradication is stated, and the reason for restoration (scientists saw the wolf as essential) is explained. ✓
  • Choice C — 'It presents a claim about the health of the Yellowstone National Park gray wolf population and then gives specific examples to support that claim.': The passage makes no claim about the health of the wolf population; it focuses on ecosystem consequences. Structure misidentified. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It explains why Yellowstone National Park allowed the eradication of the gray wolf and then discusses the consequences of reintroducing the wolf to the park.': The passage never explains why the wolves were eradicated — it simply states that they were. Structure misidentified. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
